entries.title: Phase Zero | Day 27
entries.body:
Completed the "Guided Pentest: Infrastrucutre"
Learnt that just one day of waking up late after going to bed late can FUCKING SNOWBALL INTO A WHOLE WEEK WASTED, (YEAH THAT'S WHAT DAY 25 WAS ALL ABOUT LOL)

So, no minor fuck ups as long as they are avoidable by shoo-ing away the laziness and procrastination. Cause thinking "I'll just stay up till 3AM and play God Of War, what's the worse that can happen" 6 FUCKING DAYS CAN BE WASTED.

So that's no longer in RAM, that's in the HDD now.

entries.title: Phase Zero | Day 26
entries.body:
Leart a lil more about nmap today. 
Leart mainly about NSE, and malformed scans like Null, FIN, Xmas and how they try to evade the firewalls.

Finally assembled the new workstation, decomissioned the SS SATAN, and now SS SATAN II sails in the BAY OF BENGAL.

EvoFox Ronin X75+
OffBeat IVY 
Zebronics USB hub (monitor incoming shortly)

entries.title: Phase Zero | Day 25
entries.body:
Learnt a great deal about metasploit modules and the framework as a whole today.
Learnt how to use multiple modules, and how to use them together to finally get what we want.
Learnt about meterpreter and why it is so useful.

Was fucking around for 6 FUCKING DAYS, 144 HOURS, HOLY FUCK. FUCKING BACK ON TRACK NOW.

AND THE FIRST PARTS OF THE WORKSTATION ARRIVE ON THE MORROW: 
EVO FOX X75 PLUS
OFFBEAT IVY
ZEBRONICS USB HUB

entries.title: Phase Zero | Day 24
entries.body:
Learnt about searching for things and ways to do show, like shodan, VirusTotal and other things.
Did a kinda semi-guided web app pentest, was fun, the only drawback being not knowing shit out of PHP.
Started THM Junior Penetration Tester Path, FEELS FUCKING AWESOME.
Got the home router installed, got a rj45 cat6 cable, feels fucking good to have ts.

GYM LOG:
Cardio: Skipping (5 minutes)                                |    Squats: 10
Crunches: 15 normal + 10 russian twists               |    Shadow Boxing: 2:30 minutes
Pushp Ups & Tricep Dips: 8 & 8                           |     Dead Hang: 37.2 Seconds
Resistance Cycling: 100 seconds                          |

entries.title: Phase Zero | Day 23
entries.body:
Learnt about data representation, how colours used to represented and how they now are represeted using hex colour codes.
Learnt about the meaning of UTF-8 and unicode and other UTFs which aren't as much frugal.
Took over a vulnerable linux machine (legally) in THM, but it was guided so no biggie.
FINISHED THM PRE-SECURITY BABY. TOMORROW WE MOST PROBABLY GET THE WIFI ROUTER INSTALLED 100Mb/s DAYM, INR 3500/3MONTHS AND WE FUCKING START JUNIOR PENETRATION TESTER PATH. FUCKING EXCITED, ALMOST GAVE JACK HIS IRON SIGHT RIFLE.

GYM LOG: Woke up dedicated, dismissed the alarm and saw: SUNDAY, July 12, 2026, and went back to sleep.

entries.title: Phase Zero | Day 22
entries.body:
Learnt about operating systems GUI and CLI difference, and not to boast, but nothing I already didn't know.
The only thing new I cam across today was the term MS-DOS, everything else was something i was already heavily familiar with in the past.

See y'all tomorrow. Captain's out!

entries.title: Phase Zero | Day 21
entries.body:
Learnt about how the kernel executes things. Systemcalls, user and kernel space. 
Learnt about the duties of the OS, bro is quite the manager.
Learnt that even the kernel relies on the drivers, it's not like one thing has all the power.
The whole stack needs to work together to make fabulous shit happen. The programs, the hardware, the kernel, the drivers, all of them.

entries.title: Phase Zero | Day 20
entries.body:
Completed "How the web works" module as well as the "Computer Fundamentals"
learnt a lot more about how a computer starts up, the POST, the bootloader and the CPU's reset instructions.
Also learnt a great deal about working of web servers like CDNs, Load Balancers, and cloud computing, AWS, Azure
and all. Was quite a productive day TBH

GYM LOG:
RUN: ~1100m                                                                        Squats: 10 | Resistance Cycling: 100Seconds
CRUNCHES: 15 normal + 10 russian twists                           Dead Hang: 31.4 seconds
Push Ups | Tricep Dips: 8                                                      Shadow boxing: (2.5kg) ~5 minutes (divided into 4 rounds)

entries.title: Phase Zero | Day 19
entries.body:
Continued doing the "HTTP in detail" room, did:                      |                         GYM  LOG:
What is HTTP(S)                                                                         |                         RUN: ~1000m
Requests & Responses                                                             |                        Crunches: 20 (felt considerably easier)
HTTP Methods                                                                           |                         Push Ups: 8 | Tricep Dips: 8 | Squats: 10
HTTP Status Codes                                                                   |                          Dead Hang: 35.1 seconds
Headers                                                                                     |                         Shadow Box: ~5 min | Resistance Cycle: 100 seconds

Started: "HTTP in detail"-->Cookies

Didn't do anything else today because i got sidelined looking for a laptop and the financing options on the P52 & P53 (thinkpads) then had a talk with my father, was trying to resolve his doubt that after failing to pass the SSB, am i in a black hole or actually working towards something. Did make him feel better.

entries.title: Phase Zero | Day 18
entries.body:
Covered these rooms in THM: Networks |  Intro To LAN | OSI Model | Packets and Frames | Extending Your Network | DNS in Detail |
Started This room: HTTP in detail

Covered mostly what i had already done in week 1, but was a good revision, some new topics came to light like: RIP, PPP, PPTP, TCP Sequence & Acknowlegement number

Learnt that i still have a long way to go. BUT IT IS GONNA BE FUN BABY.

GYM LOG:
Went to the gym, gym was closed, realized it was sunday, came back.

entries.title: Phase Zero | Day 17
entries.body:
Learnt about setting up VirtualBox, the entirety, host modules, downloading->compiling->loading->persisting
Learnt about the network internals of VirutalBox
THEN
Actually configured my own VirutalBox setup. Configured my own topology.
Troubleshooted the network not working with two network adpaters, only to find out I had to click the icon. Bruh.

GYM LOG:
1000m Run (Treadmill)
5 push ups | 5 trice dips
20 crunches
Dead Hang: 30.6 seconds
Resistance Cycling: 100 seconds
Dumb-Bell shadow boxing ~5 minutes

entries.title: Phase Zero | Day 16
entries.body:
Learnt about the different kinds of XSS, and how the browser gets blamed when bro's doing exactly what he's told to.
Finished Zaid Sabih's course today, and will complete the home lab tomorrow and get started with THM.

GYM LOG:
1000m Run (Treadmill)
5 push ups | 5 trice dips
20 crunches
Dead Hang: ~28sec
1 minute or two of resistance cycling
Dumb-bell shadow boxing ~5 minutes

entries.title: Phase Zero | Day 15
entries.body:
Learnt about the two methods of resource "logistics" for HTTP: GET & POST
Learnt that they don't just work with html pages, they work with WAY more.

Learnt about the concept of SQL Injection in web apps.
Learnt that SQL itself is quite innocent. It' s the Application that's been on marijuana.

Starting GYM from tomorrow, and -- effective immediately -- this log will now have the day's GYM routine as well. 
CAUSE WHO TF SAID TS FORMAL BITCH!!! LMAO

entries.title: Phase Zero | Day 14
entries.body:
Learnt about what pivoting is, in the context of taking over (hacking) machines.

Leart NOT to fuck with the sleep cycle, or it fucks back. The bill has to be paid.
Learnt how batman arkham asylum is a fucking great game for its time lol.

entries.title: Phase Zero | Day 13
entries.body:
Learnt how trojans can be easily defended against.
You just gotta have SOME FUCKING COMMON SENSE about you and  check whether the file is what it is, or it straight up lyin'.

Learnt different methods to make our machine publicly accessible on the internet, in fancy words: Learnt how to establish an endpoint on the public internet for your private machine.
There are WAYS man.

Learnt how much the term "Tunnel" is thrown around lol.
entries.tags: Trojan Defense,Exposing yourself to the internet

entries.title: Phase Zero | Day 12
entries.body:
Learnt about how backdoors are created, and how tools just automate things for you, msfvenom might make the payload included backdoor for you, but it shouldn't become your identity.

Learnt about the use of Maltego for information gathering, great tool, but still a tool.
Learnt about the security stack of SMTP which funny enough, also has tree entities making it actually secure just like DNSSEC in DNS

And learnt how it's not actually wise to watch vidoes seeing a guy go clicking about on a software.

entries.title: Phase Zero | Day 11
entries.body:
Learnt about attack methods. How there's client and server side attacks.
Learnt about how VPN works, like actually works, and nope, it doesn't give you internet.
Learnt about metasploit and how to use it (a little)
Learnt about bro's distant cousin NEXPOSE, which is a jack of all trades.... on steroids.

Learnt that even if my phone's battery is 7000mAh, i should still carry my charger on me.

entries.title: Phase Zero | Day 10
entries.body:
Learnt about DNS Spoofing and how it works.
Learnt how HTTPS is near impossible to spoof using DNS because of cryptographic, and 
that's why hsts hijacking exists.
Learnt about DNSSEC internals.
(THEY REALLY ARE EXTENSIONS, AIN'T ONE THING FUCKING THREE, TRES)

And then... Bought CosmicByte Ares tri mode controller and battle tested  for bluetooth, 2.4GHz dongle and wired playing on linux, and  bt over android lol. TS HAS HALL EFFECT STICKS AND TRIGGERS. TS fire lol.

entries.title: Phase Zero | Day 9
entries.body:
Learnt how traceroute does what it does. (bro quite literally traces the route)
Learnt how nmap `-Pn` works and why it is a good tool to have in hand cause at time the servers just be lyin'
Learnt enumeration methodology.
Have started to prioritize information gathering and understanding over exploits.
Learnt how every new piece of information changes the whole hypothesis (Bayesian principle)

Learnt that old bob has seen things.

entries.title: Phase Zero | Day 8
entries.body:
Learnt about enumeration and recon habits.
Learnt why information gathering is like 80% of penetration testing.
Learnt to be patient and keep, quite literally, poking around.
Learnt nmap internals.

Did a scan on the library router and  found out that it was running HTTPS.
From there, did a service version scan, and  found a lead from the <title> of the HTML.
Looked that up and found that DEFAULT CREDENTIALS. (took like 10 minutes, most of it gathering info)

Disclosed that to the owner and had bro change shit from default credentials to ANYTHING.
Did a lot, learnt more than I did.
(Oh and the streak gap is cause i had a medical appointment, yup, human factors LMAO)

entries.title: Phase Zero | Day 7
entries.body:
Learnt the common ports used for services.
Learnt the attack methodology used in penetration testing.
Learnt about the attack surface on each layer of the OSI model and distinguishing between each one.
Learnt about TLS, and the cryptographic protection used  for web pages such as CA certificates, public and private keys.

Took significantly less notes today, but sure as hell it was a great sail. 
WEEK ONE ENDS, BUT THE VOYAGE IS JUST BEGINNING.

entries.title: Phase Zero | Day 6
entries.body:
Learnt about the DNS records which live on the authoritative NS.
And holy fuck they don't just serve the IP they server ALL SORTS OF SHIT, it's like a fucking buffet.
Learnt about how DHCP actually works, well... i did know about lease, but learnt that the DHCP pool and total usable addresses in a subnet are two different things. The former is a subset of the latter.

Did use Wireshark today, sent ARP discovery requests from my laptop to the router and then captured the whole flow of request going and coming back through Wireshark.
SHIT FELT FIRE. FUCKING DOPAMINE FR FR.

Screenshots can be found in the github repository at: https://github.com/saffron-sh/cybersec-journey

entries.title: Phase Zero | Day 5
entries.body:
Learnt how DNS actually works.
Learnt about why /etc/hosts overrides DNS
Learnt the hierarchy of the DNS recursive search.
Learnt about the existence of registry & registrars for domains.
Learnt who TF am i paying money to when i buy a domain.
Learnt about linux 'dig' tool.
Learnt about the existence of ICANN & IANA
Learnt why tf a messed up sleep cycle can lead to a sub-optimal level of productivity day.
And DMAN, it's not like the DNS just gives you the IP addresses, bro has to ask, fucking thrice LMAO.
TS GOT LEVELS TO IT, LITERALLY.

entries.title: Phase Zero | Day 4
entries.body:
Learnt about Subnets
Learnt about the CIDR notation
Learnt about How the LAN is divided internally. AND HOLY FUCK THERE ARE WAYS.

Learnt how to quickly find the Broadcast and the Network IP using the "Octet Blocks" method
TS GOLD FR FR
Understand both the old school binary way of understanding and translating Subnets as well as the modern, quick method which used octet block method.

entries.title: Phase Zero | Day 3
entries.body:
Learn a SHIT TON about how ONE packet goes from my browser all the way to the servers of the host i am trying to reach. AND MIND YOU IT'S A FUCKING GREAT JOURNEY LMAO.
Learnt about TCP internals
Learnt about UDP internals
Learnt about IP FUCKING INTERNALS (yeah direct fucking connection to the first sentence)
Learnt about why ROUTERS are quite litreally ROUTERS lmao. 
They route packets from HOP to HOP
Learnt about TTL. THIS IS FUCKING AMAZING

entries.title: Phase Zero | Day 2
entries.body:
Learnt about how NAT actually works, and what it stores.
Learnt the distinction between ports and sockets.
Learnt about ephemeral ports
Learnt ss basics
Learnt How packets actually travel across the internet, they don't just go to google, they go from source->router->router->router->a port of the destination.
Learnt how processes in linux care about sockets, ports are just like always existing locations, and sockets are what actually turn them from dormant to active.

entries.title: Phase Zero | Day 1
entries.body:
Learnt the OSI model, how things work in it.
Learnt how each layer works with the other in unison, it's not like they layers are too different from each other. 

THEY WORK TOGETHER, YEAH.

Learnt that DNS is layer 7, not layer 4.
Learnt that layer 7 (application)creates an HTTP request, layer 4 (transport) puts the source port and the destination port in the TCP header and the paylod becomes the IP packet which is in turn created at layer 3, Network layer, which is put in the Ethernet frame created at layer 2, it contains a source MAC, and the destination MAC (your router), and then the layer 1, the physical cable, pushes it into bits and it traverses as light signals.
